items.title: Stage-specific requirement for METTL3-dependent m<sup>6</sup>A epitranscriptomic regulation during myogenesis
items.author: Shu-Juan Xie
items.content: Function and mechanism underlying m6A modifications in myoblast differentiation and skeletal muscle regeneration while elucidating the regulatory role of METTL3 in promoting successful myoblast fusion.

items.title: Pooled CRISPR screens identifies key regulators of bovine stem cell expansion for cultured meat
items.author: Roni Rak
items.content: Pooled CRISPR knockout screening in bovine mesenchymal stem cells identifies TP53 and PTEN as key regulators of proliferation, offering a genetic strategy to enhance cell expansion and address scalability challenges in cultured meat production.

items.title: An effective system for senescence modulating drug development using quantitative high-content analysis and high-throughput screening
items.author: Peichuan Zhang
items.content: Fluorescent detection of senescence-associated beta-galactosidase (SA-beta-gal) activity, enabling automated high-content drug screening and IC50/EC50 measurement, facilitates senescence research and drug development.

items.title: Histone H3K27 acetylation mediated by KAT8 maintains antiviral trained immunity in shrimp induced by inactivated white spot syndrome virus
items.author: Jin-Xing Wang
items.content: Molecular analysis of virus-trained shrimp reveals that KAT8-mediated H3K27ac drives antiviral memory through metabolic rewiring and Dorsal transcription factor activation, enhancing production of interferon-like Vago5 and antimicrobial peptides.

items.title: A single-cell atlas of mouse central nervous system immune cells reveals unique infection-stage immune signatures during the progression of meningitis caused by Streptococcus suis
items.author: Liancheng Lei
items.content: SS2 meningitis has driven unique infection-stage immune cells emerging with novel phenotypes in mouse brain including myeloid cells and lymphocytes.

items.title: Gesture encoding in human left precentral gyrus neuronal ensembles
items.author: Leigh R. Hochberg
items.content: Single-unit ensemble activity recorded in unilateral human precentral gyrus reveals a wide range of gesture-related signals across both hands, providing an intuitive and diverse set of potential command signals for intracortical BCI use.

items.title: Overcoming platinum-resistant ovarian cancer targeting the activated JAK-STAT pathways via extracellular vesicles
items.author: Hiroaki Kajiyama
items.content: Peritoneal mesothelial cell-derived extracellular vesicles provide a new mechanism for platinumresistant ovarian cancer through the enrichment of miR-135a-5p, which activates JAK-STAT signaling and suggests JAK as a key therapeutic target.

items.title: TRIM2 inhibits apoptosis by ubiquitinating BNIP3 to protect the intestine against ischemia-reperfusion injury in mice
items.author: Xuekang Zhang
items.content: TRIM2 protects male mice from intestinal ischemia-reperfusion injury by marking the pro-death BNIP3 protein for degradation, revealing a potential therapeutic target.
